title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "National Primary Drinking Water Regulations for Lead and Copper: Improvements (LCRI)".
plain_summary: This bill would cancel a new EPA rule about lead and copper in drinking water. The EPA rule was created in October 2024 to make drinking water safer by reducing lead. The rule would require water companies to replace old lead pipes within 10 years. If Congress passes this bill, the EPA rule would be thrown out and have no power. This means water companies would not have to follow the new requirements to replace lead pipes.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress officially rejects a rule created by the Environmental Protection Agency. This rule was called National Primary Drinking Water Regulations for Lead and Copper Improvements. The EPA published this rule in the Federal Register on October thirtieth, two thousand twenty four. Because Congress is rejecting this rule, it will not become law and will have no legal power.
